---
title: Quickstart
description: Get to a working Elata app quickly with the supported developer entrypoints.
---

This page is the shortest path from "what is this SDK?" to a working local app.

## Fastest Path

If you are evaluating the SDK for the first time, scaffold a demo app:

```bash
npm create @elata-biosciences/elata-demo my-app
cd my-app
pnpm install
pnpm run dev
```

Available templates (recommended priority):

- `rppg-web-demo`: **primary** — camera-based rPPG demo
- `eeg-web-demo`: **optional** — browser EEG WASM demo with synthetic data
- `eeg-web-ble-demo`: **Bluetooth** — EEG demo with Muse-compatible Web Bluetooth

## Which Template Should You Choose?

| If you want to... | Start here |
| --- | --- |
| Build the default camera-based app (no headset) | `rppg-web-demo` |
| Add browser EEG without a headset yet | `eeg-web-demo` |
| Turn on Bluetooth for a Muse-compatible headset (Chrome/Edge) | `eeg-web-ble-demo` |

## What You Are Choosing Between

- **rPPG**: pulse estimation from a camera stream — the **usual first app**.
  Choose this when you want biosignals without hardware or when you are still
  exploring the SDK.
- **EEG**: brain-signal analysis in the browser — **optional** until your
  product needs headset-driven or neuro-style features. Start with `eeg-web-demo`
  for WASM and synthetic data before worrying about hardware.
- **Web Bluetooth (`eeg-web-ble-demo`)**: **enables a live Muse-compatible
  headset** on top of the EEG stack. It is not a separate product category from
  EEG; it is how you connect the device in supported browsers.

## Existing App Path

If you already have an app and know which capability you need, install the
published package directly:

```bash
pnpm add @elata-biosciences/rppg-web
pnpm add @elata-biosciences/eeg-web
pnpm add @elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble
```

Use only the packages your app actually needs.

The common paths are:

- `@elata-biosciences/rppg-web` for browser camera-based rPPG (typical first integration)
- `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web` for browser EEG analysis APIs
- `@elata-biosciences/eeg-web-ble` plus `eeg-web` to **enable** live Muse-compatible Bluetooth streaming

## App-Level Rule Of Thumb

- Start with `rppg-web-demo` for the default camera-based app (no headset).
- Add `eeg-web-demo` when you want browser EEG without Bluetooth yet.
- Use `eeg-web-ble-demo` when you need **Bluetooth on** for a real Muse-compatible headset in Chrome or Edge.

## Recommended Decision Order

1. Start with `@elata-biosciences/create-elata-demo` for evaluation or a new app.
2. Install a published package directly when integrating into an existing app.
3. Use repo-internal workflows only if you are modifying the SDK itself.

## What Not To Start With

- Do not start with `./run.sh sync-to` unless you are actively developing `packages/eeg-web` in this monorepo.
- Do not assume the in-repo demos are the default consumer path.
- Do not treat a parent-workspace `pnpm` install issue as a package failure until you check the workspace caveat below.

## Workspace Caveat

If you scaffold an app inside another `pnpm` workspace and the new app is not
part of that workspace, install and run it from the parent directory:

```bash
pnpm --dir my-app --ignore-workspace install
pnpm --dir my-app --ignore-workspace run dev
```

Or use `npm install` and `npm run dev` inside the generated app.
